title: Example: SEC charges a crypto firm over an unregistered offering
dek: This is an illustrative example of the Crypto Cronkite format. It is not a real story.
author: The Crypto Cronkite desk
body: This page exists to show the format a finished Crypto Cronkite story takes, using an invented example. Nothing on this page is a real event.

In a real story, this is where the plain-language report goes: what a regulator did, who it names, and what it is seeking, each claim traceable to a linked primary source. The writing is straight and factual, with no hype and no urgency, and it never advises a trade.
This section explains the significance in careful, non-advisory terms. It reports what the event could signal for the market or the ecosystem, and it stops short of telling you what to do about it.
key_fact: In a real story, the single most important verified fact sits here, in one sentence.
human_take: This is the slot for the human editor's honest take. The AI never writes this line; a person does, on the record.

sources.title: Primary source (example only, not a real link)
